draft.introduction: Book 2 of the Imitation of Christ counsels the “interior life” and the cultivation of inner peace. Its central teaching is that the kingdom of God is within, and external circumstances matter less than the state of the soul.

translation.original_ref: Thomas à Kempis, Imitation of Christ
translation.text: The kingdom of God is within you, says the Lord. Turn with your whole heart to the Lord, and forsake this wretched world, and your soul shall find rest. Learn to despise outward things and to give yourself to inward things, and you shall see the kingdom of God come within you. For the kingdom of God is peace and joy in the Holy Spirit, which is not given to the unholy.
translation.original_text: [Latin: Regnum Dei intra vos est, dicit Dominus]

translator_notes.note: The emphasis on interiority draws on Augustine’s “return to yourself” (redi in te ipsum) and the Rhineland mystics. The Imitation translates the sophisticated theology of Eckhart and Tauler into language accessible to ordinary Christians, democratizing the contemplative tradition.
